projectDescription:
A desktop application developed as a 3rd-year core programming project. Hospit-Track was designed to solve the critical problem of hospital bed allocation and resource management in real-time in the peak of COVID crisis.

The application features an interactive analytics dashboard that visualizes bed occupancy across different departments (Public vs. Private wards) using dynamic charts. I implemented a request-handling workflow where patient admissions are queued, reviewed, and assigned to specific beds based on availability. This project highlights my proficiency in handling stateful data, and building business workflows within a Java environment.This system was developed for my Java programming subject. It represents my ability to present information clearly to end-users through visualization.

A self-initiated project developed during my 3rd year of college to master advanced Java Swing concepts and software architecture. Costa-LMS is a modern desktop application designed to streamline library operations, featuring a sleek, custom-built UI that goes far beyond standard Java library defaults.

The system implements complex library workflows including book issuance (with availability checks), automated return processing, and a 'Defaulter Tracking' system to identify overdue books in real-time. By utilizing custom UI components and a clean modular design, this project demonstrates my ability to take a standard business requirement and elevate it into a professional, production-ready software solution.

projectTitle: PUPFind: Real-Time Lost & Found Ecosystem for Campus Communities
projectSlogan: Streamlining Campus-Wide Item Recovery via Socket.io
projectDescription:
PUPFind is a real-time, full-stack retrieval system developed as a university thesis project to solve the fragmented lost-and-found process at the Polytechnic University of the Philippines.

The platform automates the matching of 'Missing' and 'Found' item reports, verified through a central administrative office. Key technical features include real-time chat via Socket.io for instant verification, secure JWT-based authentication, and an automated matching workflow that links data models to facilitate item recovery. The system also includes a robust admin suite for generating official retrieval receipts and analyzing campus lost-item hotspots. This project was awarded high technical marks for its implementation of complex real-time data synchronization and its social utility within the campus community.

projectSlogan: Collaborate and solve coding challenges together
projectDescription:
LeetCollab is an innovative online platform built for collaborative coding and algorithm problem-solving. It enables users to tackle coding challenges together in real-time, fostering a dynamic learning environment. LeetCollab provides a rich set of collaboration tools, including a live chat feature for seamless communication and an interactive whiteboard for real-time visual problem-solving and diagramming. The platform also supports a shared coding environment, where users can simultaneously write, edit, and run code, making it an ideal space for pair programming, study groups, and algorithm practice.

Whether you're preparing for coding interviews or simply honing your problem-solving skills, LeetCollab offers a community-driven experience designed to make learning more interactive and engaging.

projectSlogan: Official Website for PCA Internal Audit and audit questionnaires
projectDescription: The official website for the Internal Audit Department of the Philippine Coconut Authority (PCA). The platform streamlined audit operations by providing a secure login system for department focal persons nationwide, enabling them to answer auditor-issued questionnaires and upload supporting documents. I also implemented a document validation workflow, allowing auditors to review, accept, or reject submissions based on completeness and compliance. This system digitized and centralized PCA's audit preparation process, significantly reducing manual effort, improving traceability, and fostering more efficient cross-departmental coordination.

projectSlogan: A highly scalable, event-driven ecommerce platform
projectDescription:
A highly scalable, event-driven ecommerce platform built from the ground up using a microservices architecture. Designed to handle high-traffic scenarios, the system is split into decoupled services (Auth, Product, Order, Payment, and Email) that communicate asynchronously via an Apache Kafka message broker.

To optimize developer experience and code-sharing, the entire suite—including two separate Next.js frontends (Client and Admin)—is managed within a Turborepo monorepo. The project shows persistence, utilizing PostgreSQL (with Prisma) for relational product data and MongoDB (with Mongoose) for flexible order documents. All infrastructure, including the 3-broker Kafka cluster and databases, is fully containerized with Docker for seamless local deployment.

An enterprise-grade, digital-first Library Management System (LMS) designed to bridge physical library operations with a modern online patron experience. It is split into a responsive User Portal for self-service and a data-driven Admin Dashboard for inventory and circulation management. The platform supports complete lifecycle tracking for library assets—from real-time catalog browsing and queue-based reservations to physical checkouts—and integrates Razorpay to handle digital payments for overdue fines and premium subscription tiers.
